name: Smoked Corn Stuffed Pepper
recipeIngredient: 15 ounces Canned black beans (drained and rinsed)
2 ounces Monterey Jack cheese with jalapenos (shredded, optional)
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut a thin slice from the stem end of each pepper; remove seeds and membranes; rinse.
2. Cook peppers for 5 minutes in enough boiling water to cover; drain.
3. Season inside of peppers with 1 teaspoon salt and black pepper; set aside.
4. Combine rice, beans, corn, onion, walnuts, chiles, liquid smoke, cumin, and remaining 1/4 teaspoon salt.
5. Spoon 1 cup rice mixture into each pepper.
6. Stand peppers upright in a 13 x 9-inch baking pan.
7. Cover pan with foil; bake at 350 degrees F for 20 minutes.
8. Remove cover; sprinkle peppers with cheese.
9. Cook an additional 5 minutes or until cheese is melted.
10. Garnish with jalapeno pepper slices.
Microwave Oven Instructions:
1. Cut a thin slice from the stem end of each pepper; remove seeds and membrane; rinse.
2. Season with 1 teaspoon salt and black pepper; set upside down on a paper towel to drain.
3. Combine rice, corn, onion, walnuts, chiles, liquid smoke, cumin, and 1/4 teaspoon salt in a 13 x 9-inch microwaveable casserole dish.
4. Cook on HIGH for 2 minutes.
5. Spoon 1 cup mixture into each pepper; place them back in the casserole dish.
6. Pour 1/4-inch water in the bottom of the dish.
7. Cover with vented plastic wrap; microwave on MEDIUM (50% power) for 7 minutes.
8. Uncover; sprinkle cheese on top of each pepper.
9. Microwave on HIGH for an additional 1 minute.
10. Garnish with jalapeno pepper slices.
